keyPoints: Sequential circuits use memory (flip-flops) to store past state.
Outputs depend on both the current inputs and the stored state.
The design process involves creating state tables/diagrams and deriving excitation equations.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that outputs in a Moore machine are only dependent on the current state, not the inputs.
Incorrectly minimizing the combinational logic before ensuring the state diagram is complete and correct.
Confusing the roles of the state register and the next-state logic in the overall circuit structure.
